premise: Kael Rusk, an under-ranked contract blade in the Rillgate Contract Hall, accepts a cheap salt-mine retrieval job to earn bronze rank before his sister's debt marker is sold. The mine is really a sealed ruin where a rival crew and a hidden patron are harvesting outlaw monster cores.

characters.name: Kael Rusk
characters.description: Under-ranked contract blade, late 20s. Uses iron-thread binding, a dangerous technique that turns monster pressure into short-lived weapon patterns at a bodily cost. Needs bronze rank to keep his sister Mira's debt marker inside Rillgate protection. Tactical, proud, and too quick to take the cleanest payout before anyone can use him.

characters.name: Tessa Mire
characters.description: Rival spearhand from a ranked crew. Her sponsor pushes her into the Gray Salt Mine, making her both competition and the witness Kael needs if he wants his evidence to survive guild law.

characters.name: Orin Vale
characters.description: Rillgate contract clerk who knows salvage clauses, witness rules, and rank-law loopholes. He can turn Kael's proof into legal standing only if Kael preserves admissible evidence.

characters.name: Lady Varn
characters.description: Polite contract broker and hidden ruin-rights patron. She prices the mine job low so unranked crews trigger illegal brine wards and leave ranked buyers unblamed.

lockedCharacters.name: Kael Rusk
lockedCharacters.mustHaveArc: Moves from grabbing the clean payout alone to using witness testimony and contract law as leverage.

lockedCharacters.name: Tessa Mire
lockedCharacters.mustHaveArc: Shifts from rival claimant to necessary witness whose testimony changes Kael's legal standing.

lockedCharacters.name: Orin Vale
lockedCharacters.mustHaveArc: Turns from warning Kael off the job to filing the evidence that grants provisional bronze rank.

lockedCharacters.name: Lady Varn
mustHaveTraits: uses cheap contracts as disposable triggers
lockedCharacters.mustHaveArc: Stays partially protected in Book 1 and becomes the pressure behind the next restricted contract.

requiredBeats.description: MPA-01 Hub pressure: Kael learns Mira's debt marker will be sold unless he gains bronze rank or equivalent leverage before the next contract bell.
mustInclude: Orin Vale warns Kael that law, not courage, decides rank protection

requiredBeats.description: MPA-02 Contract offer: Lady Varn posts a cheap Gray Salt Mine retrieval job with clear terms, low pay, and one minimized risk around brine ward activity.
MPA-03 Crew friction: Tessa's ranked crew claim collides with Kael's unranked bid, forcing a witness/salvage problem before the mine opens.
mustInclude: ally or rival pressure changes the job plan

requiredBeats.description: MPA-04 Arena entry: The flooded Gray Salt Mine immediately constrains tactics with brine wards, rank-token responses, and unsafe salvage routes.
MPA-05 First tactical win: Kael uses iron-thread binding at a named bodily cost to win information or a resource without solving the job.
MPA-06 Job complication: The retrieval target proves to be part of an illegal ruin-rights harvest, not the harmless mine recovery described on the contract.
mustInclude: mission target is not what the contract claimed

requiredBeats.description: MPA-07 Progression trial: Kael must refine iron-thread binding under pressure, choosing between a stronger technique and a worse lingering injury.
MPA-08 Faction reveal: Evidence links Lady Varn's brokers to a larger ruin-rights buyer, making the job a faction problem rather than a mine accident.
mustInclude: larger conflict exposed but not resolved

requiredBeats.description: MPA-09 Contract climax: Kael completes, subverts, or renegotiates the objective by saving Tessa as a witness instead of taking the clean core payout.
mustInclude: evidence survives because Kael refuses the clean payout

requiredBeats.description: MPA-10 Return and next hook: Kael returns to Rillgate with contested proof, gains provisional bronze status, protects Mira, and receives a restricted next contract tied to Varn's buyers.
mustInclude: status changes and next mission pressure appears

forbidden: Do not make Kael a chosen-one heir.
Do not add stat sheets or explicit LitRPG interfaces.
Do not position Book 1 as harem, academy, or base-building.
Do not resolve the entire ruin-rights faction problem in Book 1.
Do not let worldbuilding remain decorative; guild law, mine rules, or monster ecology must constrain choices.

structuralConstraints.povRotation: Single POV through Kael Rusk unless the planner has a concrete reason to use a brief alternate witness perspective.
structuralConstraints.pacing: One contained paid mission: hub pressure, contract, crew friction, arena, tactical win, complication, progression trial, faction reveal, climax, return hook.

storyThreads.label: Rillgate contract loop
storyThreads.description: Paid contracts, rank law, salvage rights, and witness rules decide who gets paid and who gains standing.

storyThreads.label: Iron-thread progression
storyThreads.description: Kael's technique improves only when he pays a named bodily cost under monster pressure.

storyThreads.label: Ruin-rights patronage
storyThreads.description: Hidden patrons launder illegal ruin harvests through cheap contracts and disposable crews.

storyDebts.promiseText: Kael must earn bronze rank or equivalent legal leverage before Mira's debt marker leaves guild protection.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off through provisional bronze rank or guild leverage created by admissible contract evidence.

storyDebts.promiseText: The cheap Gray Salt Mine job must reveal why the danger, patron, or objective was hidden.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off when Kael proves the retrieval job was a rigged ruin-rights harvest.

storyDebts.promiseText: Kael and Tessa must shift from rival claimants to necessary witnesses.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off when Tessa's testimony changes Kael's legal standing and future options.

storyDebts.promiseText: The mine job must point to a stronger patron or next contract rather than resolving the whole faction problem.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off with a restricted next mission tied to Lady Varn's buyers.

storyPayoffs.payoffText: Kael returns with admissible evidence and gains provisional bronze standing before Mira's marker is sold.
The climax proves the mine retrieval was priced to trigger an illegal ruin-rights harvest.
Tessa's testimony makes Kael's claim legal instead of merely heroic.
Orin offers Kael a restricted next job tied to Lady Varn's hidden buyers.

directives.rawNotes:
Method pack: mercenary-progression-adventure-v0. Book 1 contract packet: series promise = one paid Rillgate mission changes Kael's rank, technique, ally network, and next-job pressure; protagonist progression axis = iron-thread binding gains a brine-hook pattern only through a lingering bodily cost; guild/hub rule = rank, witness, and salvage clauses decide whether action becomes legal standing; Book 1 contract = retrieve a missing survey chain and salt-sealed core from the Gray Salt Mine for low pay; arena = flooded mine over sealed ruin with brine wards responding to blood, rank tokens, and stolen cores; hidden complication = Lady Varn priced the job to make unranked crews trigger illegal wards for a patron; party shift = Tessa moves from rival claimant to necessary witness; faction consequence = Rillgate learns a polite broker is laundering ruin-rights theft through cheap contracts; next hook = Kael receives a restricted next mission aimed at Varn's buyers.
First two chapter scene pressure notes:
Ch1 scene A objectivePressure=Kael must get a bronze path before Mira's marker sale; tacticalConstraint=rank law blocks direct protection; allyMateriality=Orin explains the clause that makes evidence more useful than bravado; endpoint=Kael needs a contract, not a fight.
Ch1 scene B objectivePressure=Kael tries to buy time with a creditor; tacticalConstraint=the marker can be sold outside guild jurisdiction; worldFactPressure=Rillgate protection depends on active rank; endpoint=the next contract bell becomes a deadline.
Ch2 scene A objectivePressure=Kael evaluates Varn's salt-mine job; tacticalConstraint=low pay signals hidden danger but no ranked crew wants it; factionPressure=Varn's civil terms make the trap legal on paper; endpoint=Kael accepts because the job is the only rank path.
Ch2 scene B objectivePressure=Kael secures tools and clauses before departure; tacticalConstraint=salvage rights require a witness or bronze token; allyMateriality=Orin warns that admissible proof matters more than the retrieved object; endpoint=the job leaves the hall with a legal weakness Kael must solve.
